title: Heatwave persists across Telangana; evening showers likely in several districts - Hyderabad Mail
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Heatwave conditions are expected to continue across Telangana on June 18, with maximum temperatures likely to remain between 40°C and 42°C in north Telangana, 39°C and 41°C in east Telangana, and 38°C to 40°C in south Telangana. Hyderabad is expected to record temperatures between 37°C and 39°C.
According to the forecast, isolated to scattered rain is likely during the evening and night in Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar, Narayanpet, Gadwal, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Yadadri-Bhongir, Hanamkonda, Warangal, Mulugu, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mancherial and Komaram Bheem Asifabad districts.
Other districts, including Hyderabad, may witness isolated evening or night showers.
During the past 24 hours, intense downpours were reported in parts of Nalgonda, Suryapet and Khammam districts, particularly during the night. Isolated rainfall also occurred in Nizamabad, Jagtial, Peddapalli and Mancherial districts.
Data from the Telangana Development Planning Society (TGDPS) showed that Chintakani in Khammam district recorded the highest rainfall at 65.5 mm, followed by Konijerla at 56.3 mm.
Other notable rainfall figures included Bonakal (30.3 mm), Mudigonda (30 mm), Neelwai in Mancherial district (25 mm), Narketpalle in Nalgonda district (23.5 mm), Nadigudem in Suryapet district (21 mm) and Mellacheruvu in Suryapet district (18 mm).
Hyderabad remained dry during the period despite rainfall activity in several districts across the state.
